title: Cover module
journey: When a teacher is unexpectedly away, someone still has to be in every one of their classrooms, fast, under real time pressure. Today that means a cover manager juggling a spreadsheet, checking who's free, who's already covering too much, and who actually teaches the subject — every single morning. This feature turns that into a ranked, rules-aware suggestion: who's genuinely free, who's fair to ask next, and a clear record of who covered what. The plan for this went through a full independent design review and was accepted — the next step is actually building it.

subfeatures.name: Timetable import
subfeatures.note: Reading a school's timetable file safely, with no student data involved.

subfeatures.name: Absence recording
subfeatures.note: A manager logs a staff absence for a day or specific periods.

subfeatures.name: Cover requirement detection
subfeatures.note: Working out exactly which lessons need covering, automatically.

subfeatures.name: Eligible-cover ranking
subfeatures.note: Ranking who's genuinely free and fair to ask, factoring in subject match and recent cover load.

subfeatures.name: Cover assignment
subfeatures.note: Assigning, reassigning, or falling back to an agency cover, with a clear record.

subfeatures.name: Teacher-facing continuity
subfeatures.note: A covering teacher sees the set work and can leave a return note for the absent teacher.

subfeatures.name: Cover analytics
subfeatures.note: Totals and patterns for cover managers: how much is covered internally vs. by agency.

testing.fixtureNote: No dedicated fixture coverage yet — this feature hasn't been built, so there's nothing to test against yet.
